deltas.releaseNoteText: commentSaveTriggered event — fires immediately when save button clicked, before async save completes. Type: CommentSaveTriggeredEvent { annotationId: string, metadata: VeltEventMetadata }. React: useCommentEventCallback('commentSaveTriggered'). Other: commentElement.on('commentSaveTriggered').subscribe(...)
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the `commentSaveTriggered` event, which fires immediately when the save button is clicked — before the async database write completes — and contrast it with `commentSaved` (post-persist). Document the `CommentSaveTriggeredEvent` payload interface (`annotationId: string`, `metadata: VeltEventMetadata`), both the React hook pattern (`useCommentEventCallback('commentSaveTriggered')`) and the non-React subscription pattern (`commentElement.on('commentSaveTriggered').subscribe(...)`), and clarify when to use each event (`commentSaveTriggered` for immediate UI feedback, `commentSaved` for reliable side-effects after persistence).

deltas.releaseNoteText: commentToolClicked and sidebarButtonClicked event aliases — past-tense aliases for commentToolClick and sidebarButtonClick. Types: CommentToolClickedEvent extends CommentToolClickEvent, SidebarButtonClickedEvent extends SidebarButtonClickEvent.
deltas.summary: Create a new rule documenting the `commentToolClicked` and `sidebarButtonClicked` past-tense event aliases (introduced in v5.0.2-beta.2) alongside the `commentToolClick` and `sidebarButtonClick` present-tense originals. Document `CommentToolClickedEvent` (extends `CommentToolClickEvent`) and `SidebarButtonClickedEvent` (extends `SidebarButtonClickEvent`), both subscription patterns, and establish the convention: prefer the past-tense aliases (`commentToolClicked`, `sidebarButtonClicked`) in new code as they follow the consistent naming pattern used by other Velt events.
deltas.ambiguityNote: The release note documents the type names and alias relationship but does not include code subscription examples. The subscription pattern is fully inferable from the `useCommentEventCallback` and `.on().subscribe()` conventions established by all other Velt events. Payload fields of `CommentToolClickEvent` and `SidebarButtonClickEvent` are not enumerated in the release note; the rule should note that the event payloads match the parent types and reference the data-models documentation for field details.

itemsEvaluated.text: commentSaveTriggered event — fires immediately when save button clicked, before async save completes. Type: CommentSaveTriggeredEvent { annotationId: string, metadata: VeltEventMetadata }. React: useCommentEventCallback('commentSaveTriggered'). Other: commentElement.on('commentSaveTriggered').subscribe(...)
itemsEvaluated.rationale: New event with explicit name, documented payload interface (`CommentSaveTriggeredEvent { annotationId: string, metadata: VeltEventMetadata }`), and both subscription patterns specified. No existing skill rule covers this event. The semantically distinct behavior (fires before async save, for immediate UI feedback) versus `commentSaved` (fires after persistence, for reliable side-effects) makes developer guidance valuable. The sister event `commentSaved` already has its own rule (`permissions-comment-saved-event.md`); a parallel companion rule is warranted. Confidence: high — all API details are explicit in the release note.

itemsEvaluated.text: commentToolClicked and sidebarButtonClicked event aliases — past-tense aliases for commentToolClick and sidebarButtonClick. Types: CommentToolClickedEvent extends CommentToolClickEvent, SidebarButtonClickedEvent extends SidebarButtonClickEvent.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: Two new event aliases with documented type names and inheritance relationships. No existing skill rule teaches `commentToolClick`, `sidebarButtonClick`, `commentToolClicked`, or `sidebarButtonClicked`. A rule documenting both the alias names and the recommendation to prefer past-tense aliases in new code provides clear developer guidance on the canonical event names. Confidence: medium — subscription patterns are inferable from convention, but the payload field details of the parent types (`CommentToolClickEvent`, `SidebarButtonClickEvent`) are not enumerated in the release note.

itemsEvaluated.text: Angular 20+ compatibility — no API change, internal context discovery fix.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: Internal framework-compatibility fix. No new API methods, props, hooks, events, parameters, or wireframe names introduced. The guardrails explicitly exclude internal refactors.

itemsEvaluated.text: Auto-exit focused thread mode — UX only, no API.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: UX-only behavior change. No API surface change. Guardrails exclude UX-only changes without API surface changes.

itemsEvaluated.text: Window scroll position preserved — UX only, no API.
itemsEvaluated.rationale: UX-only behavior change. No API surface change. Guardrails exclude UX-only changes without API surface changes.

itemsEvaluated.text: Boolean attribute handling for comment-pin-selected and full-expanded — now accepts '' (empty string).
itemsEvaluated.rationale: Bug fix restoring correct HTML boolean attribute behavior. No new API methods, props, hooks, or events introduced. The accepted prop values were always intended to be boolean-compatible; this fix restores the correct behavior. Guardrails explicitly exclude bug fixes unless they change documented behavior taught by a skill rule — no skill rule teaches this prop-level behavior.
Zero changes to Notifications or CRDT in this release. Both skill sets require no updates.
